data.title: AI-Powered Shopping to Become Mainstream by 2026, Says Visa Executive
data.description:
AI-assisted shopping is expected to become mainstream by 2026, according to Oliver Jenkyn, President of Visa's Global Market Group. As partnerships between payment giants and AI companies accelerate, interactive shopping on platforms like ChatGPT will transition from niche to everyday use for consumers.

Jenkyn highlighted that while chatbot-based shopping features will be available by 2025, the real turning point will come the following year. AI-powered shopping assistants will significantly influence purchasing decisions, although full AI-driven transactions—where AI handles payments and purchases—will take longer to gain widespread acceptance.

The delay is not due to technology but consumer habits. Many users still prefer to maintain control over final payments after receiving AI recommendations. Jenkyn noted that AI-driven commerce would initially penetrate standardized, high-frequency purchases like laundry detergent and coffee creamer, where trust barriers are lower.

In response to this shift, global payment giants are positioning themselves strategically. Visa is in discussions with major industry players, while Mastercard has partnered with Microsoft, and PayPal plans to integrate its digital wallet with OpenAI's chatbot.

This stock alert was generated using automated technology and GuruFocus financial data to provide readers with timely and accurate market reporting. This content was reviewed by GuruFocus editorial team prior to publication. Please send any questions or comments about this story to [email protected].

data.title: Apple Wins Partial Reversal of Sanctions in Epic Games Antitrust Case
data.description:
Apple (AAPL, Financials) won a partial success in its antitrust case with Epic Games when a U.S. appeals court lowered fines that limited charges to developers while maintaining a broad injunction to increase App Store competition.

An April contempt ruling against Apple was too wide, according to the 9th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals. Apple should be entitled to argue for a fair fee on sales made outside the App Store when customers click app links, the court ruled. The court maintained most of the contempt judgment and upheld an order directing Apple to remove developer restrictions.

Epic Games sued Apple in 2020 over iOS app distribution and in-app payments. A 2021 injunction ordered Apple to provide connections to other payment methods notwithstanding its trial victory.

Apple charged 27% on select off-app purchases instead of 30% on the App Store after the adjustment. Epic claimed the policy breached the injunction, resulting in a contempt decision and fee suspension.

According to Apple, the injunction should only apply to Epic. The appeals court disagreed. The judgment maintains pressure on Apple's App Store revenue while platform fee regulation persists.

data.title: Rivian Unveils New Autonomy Chips, but Investors Focus on the Bill
data.description:
Rivian Automotive (RIVN) outlined a broad push into artificial intelligence and self-driving features during its first Autonomy and AI Day on Thursday.

The update came as the stock fell more than 6%, part of a wider pullback in AI-linked names.

Executives said the company has developed a new in-house processor designed to offer roughly four times the computing power of its current system, replacing earlier chips supplied by Nvidia (NVDA, Financial). The move echoes Tesla's (TSLA, Financial) effort to design its own AI hardware.

Rivian plans to add lidar, a laser-based sensor used to map surroundings, to its upcoming R2 midsize SUV arriving in 2025. The system will join additional cameras and radars in an effort to bolster safety and support Rivian's next wave of driver-assistance features.

CEO R.J. Scaringe said Rivian expects hands-off, eyes-on driving to expand to more than 3.5 million roads by year-end. The company aims to introduce point-to-point navigation in 2025, followed later by eyes-off capability, a target also pursued by General Motors (GM, Financial).

Rivian is also preparing an AI assistant due in early 2026 and a new subscription plan, Autonomy+, priced at a one-time $2,500 fee or $49.99 per month. The company said both offerings may help generate recurring revenue as it invests in scaling production and autonomy software.

data.title: Citron Says SentinelOne Still Undervalued After Shift to AI-Native Platform
data.description:
SentinelOne, Inc. (S, Financials) remains undervalued even after moving from a pure endpoint security provider to a broader AI-native data and security platform, according to new commentary from Citron Research.

The firm said the company's pivot has expanded its relevance across cloud, data and AI security, yet the stock has fallen more than 30% this year. Citron argued that the market is discounting the company's execution and its role in several high-profile cyber investigations, which have strengthened confidence in its technology.

SentinelOne's Purple AI platform has become a key differentiator, offering embedded generative AI tools for threat detection and response. Citron noted that the platform was not compromised during the Salt Typhoon breach, a contrast to what it called vulnerabilities across some legacy infrastructure vendors. That performance could help SentinelOne win share as enterprises reassess their security dependencies.

The firm also highlighted growth in emerging products, which now account for roughly half of new contracts. Citron said strong ROI metrics and efficiency gains in these areas support a higher valuation and set the stage for improved growth over the next several quarters.

Investors will be watching upcoming earnings for signs that new products are lifting revenue and whether the company can close the gap between its technology positioning and its current market valuation.

data.title: OpenAI Rolls Out GPT-5.2 as It Races to Extend AI Lead
data.description:
OpenAI released GPT-5.2, its latest flagship model, in one of its quickest turnarounds to remain ahead in the fast-moving AI business.The business stated GPT-5.2 would come in Instant, Thinking, and Pro versions for distinct reasoning and speed levels. Initial benchmarks demonstrate improvements over GPT-5.1 in structured tasks, software engineering, and long-form thinking. To reduce computation expenses, developers might produce more each token.

The speedy release shows how aggressively OpenAI is advancing as Anthropic, Google, and others release high-end models. The upgrade improves knowledge work and complicated agentic code, which are key to corporate AI adoption, executives added.

OpenAI uses Microsoft's cloud infrastructure and Nvidia GPUs to scale the new model. That connection could make GPT-5.2 more accessible across corporate tools and developer platforms, possibly boosting the company's reach at a time when multimodal and reasoning-heavy systems are in demand.

Investors and analysts will examine how fast GPT-5.2 is adopted and if OpenAI continues its rapid development pace in the following cycle.

data.title: Lululemon Sets $11B 2025 Target as CEO Exit Nears
data.description:
Lululemon athletica inc. (LULU, Financials) is raising its revenue goal to $11 billion for 2025, a move that comes as the company prepares for a leadership change and works through mixed demand across key markets.

Calvin McDonald, the CEO, announced he would step down at the end of January after working for the firm for more than seven years. His choice comes at a time when Lululemon's overseas business is becoming more important, notably in China, where sales rose 46% in the third quarter. That strength has helped make up for a sluggish U.S. economy, when traffic slowed down after Thanksgiving and sales rose up.

Executives said that the U.S. is facing a number of problems, such as rising tariffs, the elimination of the de minimis import break, and bigger markdowns as the firm sells off its seasonal stock. Those things have made it harder to make money and have made the conclusion of the year more difficult.

Management claims, nevertheless, that it is focused on making long-term changes. People are working to make assortments simpler, get new products into the pipeline, and make the whole company run more smoothly. Leaders claim the corporation is now in a stronger position for next year, but most of those adjustments won't show up in results until spring 2026.

Investors will be looking for further information about the CEO change and if overseas growth can keep up with problems in the U.S. in the next earnings report.

  • CEO Buys, CFO Buys: Stocks that are bought by their CEO/CFOs.
  • Insider Cluster Buys: Stocks that multiple company officers and directors have bought.
  • Double Buys: Companies that both Gurus and Insiders are buying
  • Triple Buys: Companies that both Gurus and Insiders are buying, and Company is buying back.

data.title: Costco Plans More Than 30 New Warehouses a Year as Digital Tools Boost Efficiency
data.description:
Costco (COST, Financials) is accelerating its growth plans, telling investors it expects to open more than 30 warehouses a year as part of its long-term expansion strategy.President and CEO Ron Vachris said the firm established eight new stores in the first quarter, increasing its worldwide total to 921, and is on target for 28 net new openings in fiscal 2026.

To keep up, the business is converting stores creatively, expanding internationally, and hiring more site development teams. Costco aims to invest $6.5 billion in new facilities and enhancements yearly.

Costco integrates digital technologies into their operations with physical development. To accelerate checkout, the business introduced digital scanning, pre-scanning, and its Digital Wallet. AI techniques are also improving pharmacy and gasoline inventory management. Better profits, smoother operations, and quicker customer flow are showing up, management said.

As more sign-ups went online, renewal rates dropped somewhat, but membership trends remain high. Costco is targeting communication and digital engagement to keep members engaged and handle growing SG&A healthcare expenditures.

The message to investors is clear Costco will aggressively grow using technology to scale efficiently. These programs' ability to offset growing expenses and declining membership growth will be critical next year.

data.title: Alphabet Faces Potential EU Antitrust Fine Over Search Self-Preference
data.description:
Alphabet's Google (GOOGL, Financials) is poised to get an antitrust fine from the EU next year because regulators believe the company hasn't done enough to stop ranking its own services first in search results.

The European Commission charged Google for violating the Digital Markets Act in March by favoring Google Shopping, Google Hotels, and Google Flights over rivals. The law prohibits huge tech corporations from favoring their own products and services.

Since the complaints, Google has proposed many search result changes.The October idea was the latest. The concepts don't fulfill DMA criteria, regulators said. Google may avoid a sanction if it makes further changes before the ruling, insiders said.

Washington has focused on the problem, claiming EU technology laws unjustly affect American enterprises. Google has warned that such changes may benefit intermediaries at the expense of European direct-to-consumer sellers.

A company that violates the DMA might be penalized 10% of its global revenues. The self-preferencing test is different from the Google Play probe that might result in sanctions.

data.title: iRobot Stuns Wall Street as Stock Jumps Nearly 50% in Hours
data.description:
iRobot (IRBT, Financial) climbed more than 48% on Wednesday as investors returned to the heavily shorted robotics maker.

Trading volume moved well above typical levels. More than 80 million shares changed hands with hours left in the session, compared with an average of about 11 million. The stock touched an intraday high near $5.11.

Market data shows short interest remains high at roughly 39% of the float, placing the company among the most shorted names in the consumer technology segment. The elevated position has contributed to sharp price swings in recent weeks.

The company has not issued a press release or regulatory filing in recent weeks that may explain the rise. Some analysts say renewed interest in companies tied to robotics and automation may be supporting the move as policy discussions in Washington continue to highlight domestic technology investment.

iRobot has been focusing on AI-driven features, deeper smart-home connectivity, and refreshed mid-range models as part of its product strategy. Investors are watching for updates on demand trends and cost controls as the company works to stabilize growth in a competitive market.
